question: A process instance is stuck with an incident because a FEEL condition uses the wrong variable (a string instead of the intended boolean). When using process instance migration to correct the mistake, what is the documented sequence of steps?

options.text: Correct the model in Modeler as a new version, deploy the new version, migrate the instance to that version, then resolve the incident.
Resolve the incident first; the engine will retry against the original definition, which automatically picks up corrections from Modeler.
Update the running process definition in place via the API; no new deployment or migration is required.
Cancel the stuck instance and start a fresh instance on the corrected process definition; migration is not applicable to incidents.

a.text: The documented flow is: correct the mistake in Modeler as a new version, deploy the new version, migrate the running instance to it, and only then resolve the incident.

b.text: Resolving the incident without first migrating just re-runs the broken condition against the same definition, producing the same incident again.

c.text: Process definitions are immutable per deployed version. Corrections require deploying a new version and migrating affected instances.

d.text: Migration is explicitly described as the way to correct mistakes in running instances without cancelling and restarting them.

explanation: Process instance migration can be used to fix incidents caused by mistakes in the process model. The documented steps are: correct the model in Modeler as a new version, deploy that new version, migrate the running instance to the corrected version, and resolve the incident. Resolving the incident before migrating just re-runs the same broken logic.
